evals.prompt: Archive this article for me: https://blockbuster.thoughtleader.school/p/the-chat-trap-why-the-smartest-ai — I want the full text in my vault, not a summary.
evals.expected_output: A full-text archive file saved to 01 - KNOWLEDGE BASE/Concepts & Ideas/ with: YAML frontmatter (source, author, publication, published, ingested, type, tags), a 3-5 sentence Summary section, a Full Article section with complete text, wikilinks throughout. Michael Simmons thinker profile updated with new Key Works entry.
assertions: File created in 01 - KNOWLEDGE BASE/Concepts & Ideas/
File contains '## Summary' section before the Full Article
YAML frontmatter includes source, author, published, ingested, type fields
Full Article section is longer than the Summary section
Michael Simmons thinker profile updated with new entry

evals.prompt: I want to scrape all posts from Michael Simmons' Blockbuster Blueprint newsletter from the last 6 months. The Substack is at https://blockbuster.thoughtleader.school
evals.expected_output: Mode 2 triggered. Skill fetches the RSS feed, counts posts, filters to last 6 months, asks for confirmation ('Found X articles. Filter: last 6 months = Y articles. Proceed?'), then after confirmation archives each article as a separate full-text MD file. Reports total archived + skipped (duplicates). Updates last_scraped on Michael Simmons thinker profile.
assertions: Skill attempts to fetch RSS feed at blockbuster.thoughtleader.school/feed
Skill asks for confirmation before fetching ('Found X articles. Proceed?')
After confirmation, at least one archive file is created
Files have type: full-text-archive in frontmatter
last_scraped updated on Michael Simmons thinker profile
Skill reports how many were archived vs skipped

evals.prompt: Save the full text of this article to my vault: https://fs.blog/feynman-technique/ — I want to be able to re-read it later.
evals.expected_output: Non-Substack URL (Farnam Street blog). Mode 1. Full article fetched, saved to appropriate KB subfolder (Mental Models or Concepts & Ideas), full text preserved with summary header. Thinker profile for Shane Parrish created or updated if he's identifiable as a recurring figure.
assertions: File created in 01 - KNOWLEDGE BASE/ (any subfolder)
File name follows pattern '[Title] — [Author Last Name].md'
